tech_stack: AWS (EC2, RDS, Lambda, Bedrock) & Microsoft Azure
AI/ML: Amazon Bedrock, LLMs, Multi-agent AI Copilot (DruAI)
CEO target: 33% of code written by AI by end-2025
pivots: Strategic partnership with Microsoft Azure (March 2025) — storing backups in Azure data centers
Launch of ReconX Labs for threat intelligence
Threat Watch product launch (January 2026) — proactive continuous threat monitoring
FedRAMP Moderate expansion for public sector (April 2025)
Shift from 'data protection' to 'cyber resilience' positioning
New CFO (Jagroop Bal) appointed February 2025 — fiscal restructuring signal
Expanded Microsoft Entra ID + Azure VM cyber recovery (November 2025)
fiscal_signals: Series H: $147M at $2B valuation (Viking Global Investors)
Surpassed $200M SaaS ARR (August 2023), estimated ~$300M current revenue
Subsidiary revenue: Druva Europe $23.1M, Druva India $49.7M (March 2025)
No new funding since Series H — operating on existing runway
Named 2025 Gartner Magic Quadrant Leader (first pure-SaaS vendor in category)
hiring_signals: CEO stated 'muted' entry-level hiring — AI replacing junior coding tasks
High demand for domain experts in data security and cloud architecture
Active hiring: ML Engineers, Cloud Security Engineers, SDETs, Engineering Managers
SVP Engineering Aalop Shah leading scaling efforts
competitive_context: Gartner MQ Leader 2025 — first 100% SaaS vendor in Backup & Data Protection
Competitors: Veeam (market share leader), Commvault (cyber resilience pivot), Cohesity+Veritas (merged entity now #1 by share)
Veeam noted as 'reactive' by Gartner — Druva has innovation positioning advantage
Cohesity-Veritas merger creates a new mega-competitor (largest by market share)

verdict.why_now: Druva is at a triple inflection: (1) Their CEO has mandated 33% AI-generated code, creating immediate demand for AI workflow modernization tools. (2) The Cohesity-Veritas merger just created a new market-share leader, pressuring Druva to accelerate innovation velocity. (3) The new CFO appointment (Feb 2025) signals fiscal restructuring — they need to demonstrate ROI on their $475M raised without burning additional runway. DataVex's Legacy Modernization AI directly addresses the AI code migration mandate, while Market Intelligence Agent can power their SDR scaling as they push the Azure partnership.
verdict.catalog_match: Legacy Modernization AI (directly supports the CEO's 1/3 AI-code mandate and microfrontend migration) + Market Intelligence Agent (to scale partner-led GTM for the Azure co-sell motion without proportional headcount)

research_trace: Phase 1: OSINT search for Druva funding/revenue — found $475M total raised, $2B valuation, $200M+ ARR growing to ~$300M.
Phase 2: Hiring trends — CEO Jaspreet Singh's AI-first mandate ('muted' hiring), demand for domain experts, ML engineers.
Phase 3: Product launches — Threat Watch (Jan 2026), Azure expansion, FedRAMP, DruAI integration.
Phase 4: Leadership mapping — CEO Jaspreet Singh, CTO Stephen Manley, CDO Milind Borate, SVP Eng Aalop Shah, new CFO Jagroop Bal (Feb 2025).
Phase 5: Competitive analysis — Gartner MQ Leader 2025, Cohesity-Veritas merger threat, Veeam market-share dominance.
Phase 6: Signal mapping to DataVex catalog — Legacy Modernization AI fits AI-code mandate, Market Intelligence Agent fits partner GTM scaling.
